get.description:
Returns a paginated page of warehouse and SaaS integration connections the caller can `use`. Connections the caller cannot access are filtered out. Use the returned `id` when creating saved-query context items. Pagination uses an opaque token returned in `nextPageToken`.

**Requires:** `context:read`

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque page token from a previous response's `nextPageToken`. Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Max items per page. Defaults to 25, clamped to 100.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by effective integration category (case-sensitive exact match).

get.description:
Returns a page of values from the item’s `content` at the given `contentKeyPath` — useful for paging through array-valued fields. Pass `nextPageToken` as `pageToken` to fetch the next page.

**Requires:** `context:read`

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: UUID of the context item (unused on the underlying reader but kept for URL consistency).

parameters.name: contextType
parameters.description: Context type of the item (required for partition-aware reads).

parameters.name: contentKeyPath
parameters.description: Dot-separated JSON path to the array of values inside `content`. Example: `enumValues` or `sample.values`.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Max values per page. Defaults to 25 (the legacy `get-context-values` action defaulted to 50; the new `clampPageSize` helper standardises every modus-api list surface on 25). Clamped to 200.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Cursor offset (numeric). Legacy semantics — prefer `pageToken` for deep pages.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque page token from a previous response's `nextPageToken`.

parameters.name: searchQuery
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring search over each value.

parameters.name: dataPathPrefix
parameters.description: Optional prefix path applied to the data-path filter before slicing values (advanced).

get.description:
Returns a page of your conversations across Modus and scopes. Use `kind=modus` for direct Modus chats only, or `kind=skills` to exclude them. Pass `nextPageToken` as `pageToken` for the next page.

**Requires:** `scopes:read`

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque page token from a previous response's `nextPageToken`. Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Legacy zero-indexed page number. Defaults to 0. Prefer `pageToken`; if both are supplied, `pageToken` wins.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Items per page. Defaults to 10, clamped to 100.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Filter by user id who participated in the conversation. Exact match against the `conversation_interactions.user_id` column.

parameters.name: toolName
parameters.description: Filter by tool name invoked in the conversation. Applied post-fetch (requires reading message blobs); use `includeTools=true` to also see the matched tools.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Filter by activity recency. Coarse server-side buckets.

parameters.name: includeTools
parameters.description: When true, populate `toolsUsed` on each list item by scanning message blobs. Defaults to false to keep the list cheap.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Filter conversations by type. `all` (default) returns every conversation; `modus` returns direct Modus chats only; `skills` excludes direct Modus chats.

Replace the payload of a run that is still queued (not yet started). Returns the run id and its current status.

**Requires:** `workflows:invoke`

Resumes an interrupted run (approval or connect interrupt) as a new turn on the same conversation. Returns Server-Sent Events. Send Accept: application/json for the polling transport.

**Requires:** `workflows:invoke`

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: Id of the interrupted run being resumed.

201.description: Polling start — returned when Accept: application/json is set. Poll GET /runs/{runId}/events for subsequent events.

get.description:
Opens an SSE connection to an already-started run. Replays events from the `Last-Event-ID` header (or from offset 0 if absent), then blocks for new events until the run reaches a terminal state (`done`/`error`/`cancelled`) or the client disconnects.

Supports multi-device watching: multiple simultaneous connections to the same `runId` all receive the same event sequence from the shared Redis Stream.

**Requires:** `workflows:read`

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description: Run identifier returned by POST /agent/runs.

parameters.name: Last-Event-ID
parameters.description: Resume cursor. Set to the `id` field of the last received SSE frame to replay only new events. Omit (or set to "0") to replay the full event history from the start.

200.description: SSE stream of run events from the requested cursor onward.

schema.description: W3C Server-Sent Events stream. Same frame format as POST /agent/runs. Ends with a terminal RunEvent (done/error/cancelled).

404.description: Run not found or does not belong to the authenticated org.

post.description:
Starts a streaming run of a scope and returns Server-Sent Events. Send Accept: application/json for the polling transport.

**Requires:** `scopes:invoke`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Scope id.

201.description: Polling start — returned when Accept: application/json is set. Poll GET /runs/{runId}/events for subsequent events.

post.description:
Executes a single workflow action step and streams the result as Server-Sent Events. Same idempotency and resume semantics as POST /agent/v1/runs: a retry with the same Idempotency-Key and body resumes the existing stream.

**Requires:** `workflows:invoke`

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Client-generated idempotency key (UUID recommended). Retry with same key + same body resumes the existing SSE stream. Same key + different body returns a 409 error event.

200.description: SSE stream of workflow action run events. Same frame format as POST /agent/runs. Ends with a terminal RunEvent (done/error/cancelled).

schema.description:
W3C Server-Sent Events stream. Each frame:
  id: <redis-stream-id>\n  data: <json-encoded RunEvent>\n\n

RunEvent shapes are identical to POST /agent/runs.

post.description:
Idempotently cancel an in-flight workflow-action run. Writes to the shared cancellation stream; the owning replica aborts the run and its upstream LLM call.

**Requires:** `workflows:invoke`

id.description: Numeric scope id (stable per-org; not reused after delete).

slug.description: URL-safe slug used in human-readable links. Unique per org.

name.description: Display name of the scope. Shown in the builder and in run logs.

description.description: Optional one-paragraph description of what this scope does.
description.example: Predicts churn risk for a customer given a 90-day usage window.

status.description: Lifecycle status. `draft` = never deployed. `active` = deployed. `deleted` = soft-deleted.

orgUuid.description: Org UUID this scope belongs to. Matches the caller’s principal.

activeVariationId.description: Active (deployed) variation id. Null until the scope is first deployed.

draftVariationId.description: Most recent draft variation id. Null when there is no draft pending.

hasUnpublishedChanges.description: True when the draft variation differs from the active variation (deploy would publish changes).

accessConfig.description: Access configuration — who in the org can use / manage this scope.

pendingOwnershipTransfer.description: Pending ownership transfer awaiting recipient acceptance. Absent when no request is in flight.

variation.description: Variation-detail payload (`toolset`, `instructions`, `model`, etc.) for the requested view. Populated by `GET /api/v1/skills/:id`; absent on list rows.

deletedAt.description: Soft-delete timestamp. Null for active scopes.

canManage.description: Whether the requesting principal can manage (edit/delete/deploy) this scope. Derived server-side from the principal + accessConfig. Present on every Scope response.

canUse.description: Whether the requesting principal can use (run, view history of) this scope. Derived server-side from the principal + accessConfig.

manageDenial.description: Why `canManage` is false, for client-facing guidance: `needs_group_manage` (shared, but no group you belong to grants Manage) or `not_owner` (private, and you are not the owner). `null` when `canManage` is true.

coreTools.description: Built-in MCP tools (chat, get_context) exposed by this scope.

mcpToolExposure.description: Outbound tool exposure for MCP clients (subset or all).

expectedOutput.description: Free-form expected-output prompt that constrains the scope’s reply.
expectedOutput.example: A 3-bullet summary highlighting churn risk drivers.

instructions.description: Ordered list of instruction blocks. Each entry is a free-form string the builder rendered into the system prompt.

model.description: Provider-prefixed model id. The orchestrator routes by prefix.

modelSettings.description: Model-specific runtime controls such as reasoning effort and context window.

toolset.description: Builder-managed toolset descriptor. Map of `{toolName: config}`; opaque to the API surface.

connectionSet.description: Connection selections (DBs, integrations) attached to the variation. Each entry is a builder-managed object; opaque here.

contextSelections.description: Context selections attached to the variation. Free-form builder objects.

evaluations.description: Evaluation entries (task + expected-output pairs) saved for this variation.

interfaces.description: Interface entries attached to the variation (chat, slack, webhook, etc.). Each is a builder-managed object.

supervisionSubordinateIds.description: IDs of scopes supervised by this scope (when it is a manager). Stringified for wire stability.

supervisionSubordinateDescriptions.description: Map of subordinateId -> manager-provided description blurb.

SuggestionEventSource.description: Product surface that emitted the event.

skill_id.description: Numeric scope id this suggestion is scoped to.

prompt.description: Prompt sent when the suggestion is selected.

metadata.description: Suggestion metadata for diagnostics and ranking.

tools.description: List of tools the scope may invoke.

id.description: Stable id of the tool (matches the registry key on the workflow runtime).

enabled.description: Whether this tool is enabled for the scope. Defaults to true when absent.

config.description: Free-form tool-specific configuration (e.g. allowlists). Schema varies per tool.

newOwnerUserId.description: Clerk user id of the org member who will become the new owner.

trigger_type.description: Trigger kind. Only `schedule` validates its nested payload here; the rest are opaque.

time_of_day.description: Time of day in 24-hour HH:MM format. Required for hourly/daily/weekly/monthly; ignored for custom.

weekdays.description: Required for weekly schedules. Lowercase day names.

day_of_month.description: Required for monthly schedules. Calendar day 1-31.

cron_expressions.description: Required for custom schedules. Each entry is a 5-field POSIX cron expression.

cron_description.description: Human-readable description of the custom schedule. Required when frequency=custom.

name.description: New display name for the workflow.

type.description: Updated workflow type. Rarely changed after creation.

description.example: Generates a daily sales report broken down by region.

trigger.description: Updated trigger configuration. Schedule shape is fully validated; other trigger types are opaque.

accessConfig.description: Updated access configuration. The service rejects changes that would lock the caller out of use + manage.

contextType.description: Required when `content` is supplied — selects which partition to update against. Free-form string (e.g. `note`, `saved_query`).

content.description: Replacement type-specific JSON content. Shape varies by `contextType`.

topics.description: Replacement topic tags. Pass `[]` to clear all tags.

entityType.description: Replacement customer-defined entity or field type.

content.description: Replacement main textual or structured content.

topics.description: Replacement topic tags. Pass `[]` to clear all tags.

judgeModel.description: Model ID for the judge LLM

judgeGuidance.description: Custom guidance for the judge LLM

memory.description: Replacement memory text. Optional — supply when revising the text.

description.example: Predicts churn risk over a 180-day window.

expectedOutput.description: Updated expected-output summary, used when this scope is supervised.

example: You are a senior data analyst.

model.description: Updated model id. Pass `null` (explicit) to clear and use the org default.

modelSettings.description: Updated model-specific runtime controls. Pass `null` to clear.

connectionSet.description: Updated connection set. Shape is integration-specific.

contextSelections.description: Updated context selections (sources / docs the scope may read).

evaluations.description: Updated evaluation entries (task + expected-output pairs) saved for the draft variation. Replaces the prior list when present.

supervisionSubordinateDescriptions.description: Map of subordinate scope id (stringified) to the manager-provided description blurb. Replaces the prior map when present.

accessConfig.description: Updated access configuration. The service rejects changes that would lock the caller out of use + manage.

managerId.description: Re-parent the scope under a new manager (or remove its current manager when null). When this field is present, all other fields are ignored.

bucket.description: ISO8601 timestamp at the start of the bucket.

model.description: Canonicalised LLM model name. Present only when grouping by model (where it equals `group_value`); omitted on scope/workflow/interface breakdowns, where the grouped value lives in `group_value`.

group_value.description: The grouped dimension value (scope/workflow id, interface, or model).

call_count.description: Number of LLM_CALL events in this bucket for this model.

credits.description: Total credits consumed across all buckets in the window.

message.description: User message that started the run.

sessionIds.description: Conversation thread ids to check for non-terminal runs.

nextPageToken.description: Opaque token for the next page, or `null` if there are no more active runs.

sessionId.description: Opaque client-generated conversation token for continuity. The server NEVER derives the run target identity (scope/workflow/modus) from this string — target identity comes only from the request path and DTO (see RunTarget). It is used solely to thread successive turns of the same conversation together.

organizationId.description: Organization id. Ignored for authenticated callers — the server uses the principal's org.

version.description: Saved version to run (scope/workflow; ignored for Modus).

status.description: Canonical status string for the error.

message.example: Run was not found or you lack permission.

info.description: Optional domain-specific context. Shape varies per endpoint.

text.description: Background context (e.g. Slack/Teams thread + channel history) to prepend to the system prompt.

userId.description: User id for system/PAT runs (usage attribution).

fileThreadId.description: File thread ID for document-scoped execution.

workflowAction.description: Workflow action descriptor: toolId, toolName, aiDescription, runtimeScope, and any action-specific parameters.

previousOutputs.description: Outputs from earlier steps in a multi-step workflow, forwarded to the action runtime.

userTimezone.description: IANA time zone name for the executing user (e.g. "America/New_York").
